subject: Geography
title: Arctic Shipping Routes
description: A short lesson to help you apply Arctic Shipping Routes.

hook.text: The Northern Sea Route along Russia's coast cuts shipping from Asia to Europe by 40%.

buildup.text: Melting ice is opening Arctic shipping lanes for months each summer.

discovery.text: Russia is investing billions in icebreakers and Arctic ports to dominate the new route.

twist.text: Increased shipping means more oil spills, noise pollution, and disruption to marine life.

climax.text: The Arctic is becoming a highway—and the environmental cost may be enormous.

punchline.text: The shortcut climate change opened may accelerate the damage.

quiz.question: How much shorter is the Northern Sea Route compared to traditional routes?
